verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70/HSP72) is primarily a cytosolic chaperone with no canonical TM domain or secretory signal. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in stressed and malignantly transformed cells via direct binding to phosphatidylserine and phosphoinositide lipids. The C-terminal substrate-binding domain is exposed extracellularly on viable tumor cells and is recognized by the clinical-stage cmHsp70.1 monoclonal antibody. Active CAR-T, ADC, and antibody programs directly target membrane-HSPA1A. Surface localization is cell-state restricted (stress/cancer), placing this firmly in contextual/cell_state_induced rather than constitutive surface expression.
